summary: Tested 4 bug fixes for Indigo AI app. Issues 2, 3, 4 confirmed fixed via code review + UI testing. Issue 1 (MongoDB URI persistence) is STILL BROKEN — mongoUri saves as null in IDB even after successful API call.

ui_bugs.issue: MongoDB URI not persisted to IDB after Apply. After clicking Apply (API call succeeds, server logs confirm), mongoUri remains null in IndexedDB. Reloading Settings shows empty field.

integration_issues.issue: setMongoUri() in AppContext.tsx calls saveData() with stale closure (mongoUri=null before React state update). Debounced useEffect save also saves null. Empirically confirmed: Cartesia key (sync save) persists correctly; mongoUri (async API then setMongoUri) does NOT persist. Tested waiting 6 seconds after Apply — IDB still shows mongoUri=null.

action_items: Issue 1 FIX NEEDED: MongoDB URI is not being saved to IDB. Root cause: in AppContext.tsx, setMongoUri() calls saveData() synchronously after setMongoUriState() but the saveData closure still has mongoUri=null. The debounced useEffect-triggered save ALSO appears to save mongoUri=null. Suggested fix: in setMongoUri (line 1131), instead of calling saveData({mongoUri: uri}), call saveToDB('indigo_app_data_core', ...) directly with the new uri value, OR use a ref to track the pending mongoUri for saveData.
critical_code_review_comments: AppContext.tsx line 1131-1133: setMongoUri calls saveData() but saveData uses stale closure (mongoUri=null). The direct saveData call (with argument ignored) and the debounced save both run with mongoUri=null. The pattern works for sync setters (e.g., setCartesiaApiKey) because there is no async gap, but for handleApplyMongoUri in SettingsScreen (which awaits an API call before calling setMongoUri), there is likely a stale closure race condition. Compare: the Cartesia key save (sync) works, the MongoDB URI save (async API call first) does not.

success_rate.frontend: 75% (3/4 bug fixes confirmed working, 1 still broken)

test_credentials: No auth required. App is open access.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Issues 2, 3, 4 are confirmed fixed. Issue 1 (MongoDB URI persistence) is still broken. Test by: navigate to Settings > MongoDB Configuration > enter any mongodb:// URI > click Apply > reload > verify the field is populated. It will be empty. The API call succeeds (server logs show 'MongoDB URI updated to: ...'). The bug is in the IDB persistence after setMongoUri is called. Cartesia key save was used as control test (same saveData pattern but synchronous) and IT WORKS. This confirms the issue is specific to the async API call flow in handleApplyMongoUri.

rca_of_the_issue.issue: MongoDB URI not persisted to IDB (Issue 1)
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: handleApplyMongoUri (SettingsScreen.tsx line 217) makes an async fetch call. On success, it calls setMongoUri(uri) from AppContext. setMongoUri calls setMongoUriState(uri) AND saveData({mongoUri: uri}). However, saveData is a useCallback and the argument {mongoUri: uri} is IGNORED by the function signature (saveData takes no params). The direct saveData() call uses the stale closure with mongoUri=null. The debounced useEffect save (which should use the NEW saveData with mongoUri=uri after re-render) is also saving null. Empirical testing confirmed: the IDB shows mongoUri=null even 6+ seconds after Apply.
rca_of_the_issue.reproduction: 1. Open app fresh. 2. Navigate to Settings. 3. Enter 'mongodb://localhost:27017/test' in MongoDB URI field. 4. Click Apply. 5. Wait 5+ seconds. 6. Read IDB: indexedDB.open('indigo_ai_db') > app_data store > 'indigo_app_data_core' key > .mongoUri = null.
rca_of_the_issue.fix_suggestion: Option A: In AppContext setMongoUri (line 1131), after setMongoUriState, use a setTimeout(saveData, 1100) with a ref that captures the new value. Option B: Override the saveData with a version that accepts partial overrides. Option C: Use a separate IDB save call directly: saveToDB('indigo_app_data_core', {...currentCoreData, mongoUri: uri}).
